{{Short description="All of space and time and their contents}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Universe
| image = Hubble ultra deep field.jpg
| caption = The [[Hubble Ultra-Deep Field]] shows thousands of galaxies across billions of light-years.
| topics = [[Cosmology]], [[General relativity]], [[Big Bang]], [[Dark matter]], [[Dark energy]]
| age = 13.787 ± 0.020 billion years
| size = At least 93 billion light-years in diameter (observable)
}}

The '''universe''' is all of [[space]] and [[time]] and their contents, including [[planet]]s, [[star]]s, [[galaxy|galaxies]], and all other forms of [[matter]] and [[energy]]. The [[Big Bang]] theory is the prevailing cosmological description of the development of the universe.

== Observable universe ==
{{Main|Observable universe}}
The observable universe is a spherical region approximately 93 billion [[light-year]]s in diameter, centred on the observer. It contains approximately:
* 10¹¹ [[galaxy|galaxies]]
* 10²²–10²⁴ [[star]]s
* 10⁸⁰ [[proton]]s (baryonic matter)

Because [[light]] travels at a finite [[speed of light|speed]] (~3×10⁸ m/s), looking farther into space means looking further back in time.

== Composition ==
Based on the [[Lambda-CDM model]]:
* ~68% [[dark energy]] — drives accelerated expansion
* ~27% [[dark matter]] — detected only via gravitational effects
* ~5% ordinary (baryonic) matter — atoms, stars, planets, gas

== History ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Epoch !! Time after Big Bang
|-
| [[Big Bang]] || 0
|-
| [[Inflation (cosmology)|Inflation]] || 10⁻³⁶–10⁻³² s
|-
| [[Big Bang nucleosynthesis]] || 3–20 minutes
|-
| [[Recombination (cosmology)|Recombination]] (CMB released) || ~380,000 years
|-
| First stars ([[reionization]]) || ~100–400 million years
|-
| [[Galaxy formation]] || ~1 billion years
|-
| [[Dark energy]] domination || ~4 billion years
|-
| Present day || 13.8 billion years
|}

== Shape and fate ==
The shape of the universe depends on its density parameter <math>\Omega</math>:
* <math>\Omega > 1</math>: closed (spherical) — [[Big Crunch]]
* <math>\Omega = 1</math>: flat — [[Big Freeze]] (heat death)
* <math>\Omega < 1</math>: open (hyperbolic) — Big Freeze

Current observations suggest a flat universe (<math>\Omega \approx 1</math>), with accelerated expansion driven by dark energy.

== Multiverse hypothesis ==
Some theories suggest our universe may be one of many in a [[multiverse]], though this remains speculative and untestable.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Big Bang]]
* [[Cosmology]]
* [[Dark energy]]
* [[Dark matter]]
* [[Galaxy]]
* [[General relativity]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Universe| ]]
[[Category:Cosmology]]
[[Category:Concepts in astronomy]]
